findings.title: HTML comment wrapper bypass via `-->` in user text
findings.detail: In `_render_turn_md()`, USER content is wrapped in `<!-- USER_TEXT_START -->` / `<!-- USER_TEXT_END -->` HTML comments as a C1 injection mitigation. If user text contains the string `-->`, the comment closes early. Remaining user text after `-->` and the `<!-- USER_TEXT_END -->` end tag are rendered as raw Markdown, defeating the wrapping. Example: if `turn.text = "Hello --> <script>alert(1)</script> <!--"`, the wrapper becomes: `<!-- USER_TEXT_START -->\n> Hello --> <script>... <!--\n<!-- USER_TEXT_END -->` — the script content escapes the wrapper.
findings.recommendation: Escape `-->` in user text before embedding in HTML comments. Replace `-->` with `--\u003E` or use a different wrapping mechanism (e.g., Unicode non-printable markers, or wrap in a Markdown code block instead of HTML comments). Note: the JSONL canonical source is unaffected — this only impacts the human-readable Markdown companion.

findings.title: SHA-256 hash truncated to 16 hex chars (64 bits) — weak collision resistance
findings.detail: `_hash_file()` returns `hashlib.sha256(...).hexdigest()[:16]`. This truncates a 256-bit hash to 64 bits. For a single session with a few hundred turns, collision probability is near zero. But if the hash is stored long-term across many sessions for integrity comparisons, 64 bits provides only ~2^32 collision resistance (birthday bound), which is weak for archival integrity purposes.
findings.recommendation: Use the full hex digest (64 chars) or at minimum 32 chars (128 bits). The 16-char truncation saves only ~48 bytes in the footer — not worth the collision risk for an archival system. If the hash is only for per-session human verification, document this explicitly in the footer text.

findings.title: No field validation on GateTurn
findings.detail: `GateTurn` is a dataclass with no validation. Fields like `turn_type` and `content_type` are free-form strings. If a caller passes `content_type="bogus"`, downstream pipeline consumers that filter on content_type will silently skip the turn. This is a data integrity issue, not a security issue.
findings.recommendation: Add an `__post_init__` validator that asserts `content_type` is one of the documented values (`user_free_text`, `system_decision`, `ai_response`, `structured_data`). Validate `speaker` is `AI` or `USER`. Log a warning on unexpected values rather than raising.

findings.title: JSONL atomicity is TOCTOU between validate and append
findings.detail: In `log_turn()`, the temp-file validation and the actual JSONL append are separate operations. Between `json.loads(tmp.read_text())` (validation) and `open(jsonl_path, 'a').write(...)` (append), another process could modify the JSONL file. In a single-process async app this is safe, but if the archiver is ever used in a multi-process deployment, this is a TOCTOU vulnerability.
findings.recommendation: Document that GateArchiver is not safe for multi-process use. If multi-process support is needed, use file locking (portalocker) or append-validate-then-rollback pattern.

findings.title: Markdown data fields rendered without escaping
findings.detail: `_render_data_fields()` outputs `**{key}**: {value}` directly into the Markdown file. The `value` is not escaped — if it contains Markdown formatting characters (e.g., `*`, `_`, `` ` ``), they would be rendered as formatting. For structured data this is low-risk (values are typically numbers or short labels), but it violates the principle of treating all user-originated data as untrusted.
findings.recommendation: Apply Markdown escaping to values in `_render_data_fields()`, or at minimum escape leading `#`, `>`, `*`, and `_` characters.

findings.title: Missing type annotation for `gate_number` in `close_session` return
findings.detail: `close_session()` returns `dict` but the return type annotation says `dict`. The returned dict has a consistent schema (jsonl_hash, turn_count, jsonl_path, md_path) — consider using a TypedDict or dataclass for stronger typing.
findings.recommendation: Consider using `typing.TypedDict` or a small dataclass for the return type.

notes: GA-SEC-1 (HTML comment bypass) is a real injection vector in the human-readable Markdown companion. The JSONL canonical source is unaffected. GA-SEC-2 (hash truncation) is a correctness concern for archival integrity. Both should be fixed before integration. The dual-format design (JSONL canonical + MD companion) is architecturally sound and provides defense-in-depth. The atomic write pattern for log_decision is correctly implemented via save_json.
